User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the OceanGuard platform.

userType.description: Type of user (citizen, coastal resident, volunteer, disaster manager).

profilePictureUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

emergencyContact.description: Emergency contact number of the user.

alertPreferences.description: Array of alert preferences (tsunami, high tide, marine debris, flooding).

address.description: Permanent address of the coastal resident.

nearestCoastalLandmark.description: Nearest coastal landmark for the coastal resident.

frequencyAtCoastalArea.description: Frequency of visits to the coastal area by the coastal resident.

knowledgeLevel.description: Knowledge level about ocean hazards of the coastal resident.

coastalRegion.description: Usual coastal region of the volunteer.

skills.description: Array of skills/expertise of the volunteer.

emergencyEquipmentAccess.description: Array of emergency equipment accessible by the volunteer (boats, drones, first-aid kits, communication equipment).

officialEmail.description: Official email of the disaster manager.

officialPhone.description: Official phone number of the disaster manager.

officialId.description: Official ID number of the disaster manager.

idProofDocumentUrl.description: URL of the ID proof document of the disaster manager.

responsibilityRegions.description: Array of regions of responsibility for the disaster manager.

accessLevel.description: Access level of the disaster manager.

preferredDataFormats.description: Array of preferred data formats for the disaster manager (CSV, GeoJSON, API access).

AadharVerification.title: AadharVerification
AadharVerification.description: Stores verification requests when using Aadhar to authenticate.

id.description: Unique identifier for the verification request.

otp.description: One-Time Password sent to the mobile number.

otpExpiration.description: Date and time when the OTP expires.

isVerified.description: Indicates whether the mobile number has been verified.

requestTime.description: Date and time when the OTP request was made.

HazardReport.title: HazardReport
HazardReport.description: Represents a hazard report submitted by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the hazard report.

hazardType.description: Type of hazard (tsunami, storm surge, high waves, etc.).

severityLevel.description: Severity level of the hazard (low, medium, high, extreme).

mediaUrls.description: Array of URLs for media (photos, videos) associated with the report.

isVerified.description: Indicates whether the report has been verified by a disaster manager.

verificationNotes.description: Notes from disaster managers during verification process.

SocialMediaPost.title: SocialMediaPost
SocialMediaPost.description: Represents a social media post related to ocean hazards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the social media post.

platform.description: Platform where the post was made (Twitter, Facebook, YouTube, etc.).

postId.description: Identifier of the post on the social media platform.

postUrl.description: URL of the social media post.

content.description: Content of the social media post.

author.description: Author of the social media post.

postDate.description: Date and time the post was created.

location.description: Location mentioned in the social media post (if any).

hazardType.description: Type of hazard mentioned in the social media post (if any).

sentiment.description: Sentiment of the social media post (positive, negative, neutral).

engagementMetrics.description: Engagement metrics for the post (likes, shares, comments).

NeedRequest.title: NeedRequest
NeedRequest.description: Represents a request for assistance from a user during a hazard.

userId.description: ID of the user making the request.

hazardReportId.description: Optional ID of a related hazard report.

needType.description: Type of need (e.g., Water, Food, Medical, Shelter, Rescue).

location.description: Location of the person in need.

description.description: Detailed description of the situation and need.

isUrgent.description: Whether the request is marked as urgent.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership for access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores Aadhar verification requests for users. Organized as a subcollection of users for localized access and secure creation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: verificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Aadhar verification request.

definition.description: Stores hazard reports submitted by users. Organized as a subcollection of users for localized access and secure creation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: hazardReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the hazard report.

definition.description: Stores social media posts related to ocean hazards. Segregation supports QAPs and simplified security rules.

params.name: socialMediaPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the social media post.

definition.description: Stores requests for assistance from users in affected areas. Kept in a separate collection for easy querying by volunteers and managers.

params.name: needRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the need request.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs. The core strategy revolves around path-based ownership for user-related data and separate collections for global data. Aadhar verification details are stored within a subcollection of the user, ensuring data locality and ease of management. Denormalization is used to avoid complex `get()` calls in security rules.

1.  **Users Collection:** `/users/{userId}` stores user profiles.  This uses path-based ownership for simple security rules.
2.  **AadharVerification Subcollection:** `/users/{userId}/aadharVerifications/{verificationId}` stores Aadhar verification requests. Storing it as a subcollection ensures that verification data is always tied to a specific user, simplifying security rules and data management.  Crucially, it allows secure creation since the user document already exists (or will be created in the same atomic batch).  This avoids `get()` calls in the security rules.
3.  **HazardReports Collection:** `/users/{userId}/hazardReports/{hazardReportId}` stores reports created by a user.
4.  **SocialMediaPosts Collection:** `/socialMediaPosts/{socialMediaPostId}`. This segregation supports QAPs by allowing listing all posts without requiring filtering based on user roles or permissions within the rules themselves.
5.  **NeedRequests Collection:** `/needRequests/{needRequestId}`. A separate collection for assistance requests allows volunteers and managers to query all open needs without complex user-based lookups, speeding up response times.
